artifacts.summary: Implementation summary documenting 17 ported scripts, 5 updated scripts, decisions made, and follow-up items

active_projects.completion_summary: Ported 17 missing scripts from .claude/scripts/ to .opencode/scripts/ and updated 5 stale .opencode/ scripts following a dependency-driven 6-phase plan. All 22 ported/updated scripts pass syntax checks; the 3 foundational sourced scripts load correctly with expected function exports; update-recommended-order.sh is preserved as the .opencode/ active task ordering system; 3 backup files preserve the original standalone postflight implementations.

memory_candidates.content: When porting scripts between dual-system architectures (.claude/ and .opencode/), use a dependency-driven phase ordering: (1) update stale shared-dependency scripts first (update-task-status.sh, update-plan-status.sh) since other scripts call into them, (2) port foundational scripts (skill-base.sh, command-gate-*, postflight-workflow.sh) since workflow/gateway scripts depend on them, (3) port gateway/wrapper scripts that depend on Phase 2, (4) port workflow scripts, (5) port review/validation scripts, (6) run global verification. Key substitutions: .claude/ -> .opencode/, .claude/context/ -> .opencode/context/, .claude/extensions/ -> .opencode/extensions/, Agent tool -> Task tool, CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS -> OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS. Use sed for bulk path substitutions followed by manual grep for edge cases. Some scripts (orphan-detection.sh, archive-task.sh, issue-grouping.sh) are mostly path-independent and can be copied as-is.

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive research report on the orchestrator system: identifies 4 .claude/ components and 3 .opencode/ components, 3 missing architecture docs, 3-tier priority classification, 4-phase implementation plan, architectural differences, and risk assessment
Implementation plan for porting the .claude/ orchestrator system to .opencode/. 4 phases with 7 sub-phases, 8 hours total

Ported the .claude/ orchestrator system to .opencode/: 3 architecture docs, the 1129-line skill-orchestrate state machine, and the 394-line /orchestrate command. All path/tool name/env var substitutions applied; Kahn's algorithm, drift thresholds (0.70/0.30), MAX_TASKS=8, and MAX_BLOCKER_ESCALATIONS=2 preserved unchanged. All verification audits pass.

memory_candidates.content: The .claude/ agent system has TWO distinct orchestrator concepts: (1) skill-orchestrator is a thin routing skill (160 lines) for command dispatch by task_type, and (2) skill-orchestrate is a 1129-line autonomous lifecycle state machine driving /orchestrate. The .opencode/ system has only the routing skill (128 lines, smaller, lacks context-protection directives) and is missing the state machine skill, the /orchestrate command, and the orchestrate-state-machine.md architecture doc. The .opencode/agent/orchestrator.md file is mislabeled - it documents a 'Read-only chat agent' for question answering, not the orchestrator system.

When porting the orchestrator system from .claude/ to .opencode/, the dependency-driven 4-phase approach (architecture docs first, then state machine skill, then command, then verification) works well for complex multi-file ports. Sub-phases for the state machine (single-task, multi-task, drift+escalation, integration) provide independent verification gates. The skill-orchestrate SKILL.md is 1129 lines and requires careful section-by-section porting to preserve algorithmic structure (Kahn's algorithm, drift thresholds, loop guard limits) while only changing paths and tool names.

active_projects.completion_summary: Ported the .claude/ orchestrator system to .opencode/ in 7 phases (4 logical phases with sub-phases 2A-2D, 3, 4). Created 5 ported files (3 architecture docs totaling 868 lines, the 1129-line skill-orchestrate state machine, the 394-line /orchestrate command) with all .claude/ -> .opencode/ path substitutions and Agent tool -> Task tool adaptations. All verification audits pass. The orchestrator system preserves Kahn's algorithm, drift inspection thresholds, and loop guard limits unchanged.

artifacts.summary: Research report on porting synthesis domain agents: identifies that the core synthesis-agent (218 lines) is missing from .opencode/, while 24 domain-specific synthesis agents in present/founder extensions are content-equivalent. Recommended phased approach: Tier 1 (port synthesis-agent.md), Tier 2 (rewire team skills - out of scope).

Phased implementation plan to port the core synthesis-agent (218 lines) from .claude/agents/synthesis-agent.md to .opencode/agent/subagents/synthesis-agent.md. 4 phases: verify targets, write the new file, validate frontmatter/references, and smoke-test. Tier 2 skill rewiring and Tier 4 domain agent sweep are documented as out-of-scope follow-ups. Total effort: 3 hours.

Ported synthesis-agent (218-line source) from .claude/agents/synthesis-agent.md to .opencode/agent/subagents/synthesis-agent.md (220 lines). All frontmatter stripped per OpenCode standard, paths updated, structural validation 9/9 PASS.

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode's synthesis-agent pattern differs from .claude/: (1) The .claude/ team-mode skills fork a dedicated synthesis-agent that reads teammate outputs in fresh context; the .opencode/ team-mode skills do synthesis INLINE in the lead, causing lead context to grow by 7-21k tokens per team run vs ~250 tokens with the synthesis-agent pattern. (2) The .opencode/ context/reference/team-wave-helpers.md describes synthesis as an inline lead procedure (lines 232-281), contradicting the .claude/ pattern. (3) Porting the synthesis-agent requires: strip 'model: sonnet' from frontmatter, update .claude/context/ -> .opencode/context/ path references, preserve allowed-tools: Read, Write restriction.

OpenCode agent frontmatter must NOT include the 'model:' field. Per .opencode/docs/reference/standards/agent-frontmatter-standard.md, the model field causes 'Model not found' errors because OpenCode's parseModel() function splits model strings by '/', so bare aliases like 'opus' produce {providerID: 'opus', modelID: ''} which fails model resolution. OpenCode uses session model selection via the TUI model picker. When porting agents from .claude/ to .opencode/, the 'model: sonnet' / 'model: opus' / 'model: haiku' lines must be deleted. This applies to 24+ domain agents in present and founder extensions that still have the 'model: sonnet' line.

OpenCode agent frontmatter pattern: Only `name` and `description` fields are supported. The `model` field causes 'Model not found' errors because parseModel() splits on '/'. When porting from .claude/ to .opencode/: (1) strip `model:` line entirely, (2) strip `allowed-tools:` line entirely (document minimal tool surface inline in Overview instead, since OpenCode does not enforce tool restrictions via frontmatter), (3) update all `.claude/context/` path references to `.opencode/context/`, (4) update all `.claude/extensions/` to `.opencode/extensions/`, (5) replace `Agent tool`/`Agent(...)` with `Task tool`/`Task(...)`. Frontmatter is exactly 5 lines: `---` opener, `name:`, `description:`, `---` closer.

OpenCode static structural smoke test pattern for agent files: when live Task tool dispatch is not available in the agent's context, validate the agent file statically with: (1) `head -5` to verify `---` frontmatter delimiters, (2) `awk` to extract and count frontmatter fields (must be exactly 2: name, description), (3) `grep -nE '^model:|^allowed-tools:'` to confirm none of the unsupported fields exist, (4) extract all `@.opencode/path.md` references and verify each resolves to a real file, (5) `grep -n '\.claude/'` to confirm no .claude/ path leakage, (6) heading-by-heading diff against source to confirm no semantic content dropped. This pattern works for any .opencode/agent/*/port task.

active_projects.completion_summary: Ported synthesis-agent (218-line source) from .claude/agents/synthesis-agent.md to .opencode/agent/subagents/synthesis-agent.md (220 lines). Stripped model: sonnet and allowed-tools: Read, Write from frontmatter per OpenCode standard, updated all 4 .claude/context/ references to .opencode/context/, added 2 new context references for future plan-synthesis support, and documented the minimal Read+Write tool-surface convention inline (since OpenCode does not enforce allowed-tools via frontmatter). All 9 stages of the execution flow, 4 error handling sub-sections, and the output contract were ported verbatim. Static structural validation: 9/9 checks PASS. Committed (40cd95d74, a0e2fb981).

Synced 95 stale context files, copied 11 missing extension files, added 3 hook scripts with manifest updates, merged index.json (106->150 entries), and backported settings.json improvements from .opencode/ to .claude/
Fixed all 5 opencode drift gaps: agent paths in nvim/nix opencode-agents.json, hook log dirs, validation script paths, synthesis-agent registration, and rules README cleanup
